title: Purely Aromatic Brazilian Chicken Stew
description: A halal-friendly one-pot dish featuring vanilla bean paste and steam, this Brazilian chicken stew is a floral and aerated delight. It combines the richness of chicken with the subtlety of vanilla and the brightness of citrus.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in a large Dutch oven over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until it is translucent, about 5 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and cook for 1 minute, until fragrant.
Add the chicken to the pot and cook until it is browned on all sides, about 5-7 minutes.
Add the vanilla bean paste, orange juice, chicken broth, thyme, salt, and pepper to the pot.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and simmer, covered, for 25 minutes.
Remove the lid and continue to simmer for an additional 10 minutes, or until the chicken is cooked through and the sauce has thickened.
Serve the stew hot, garnished with fresh herbs if desired.
